description: The concurrent process-group delivery comment contradicts the distinct module process group.
The focused-runner concurrent process-group delivery comment contradicts the distinct module process group.
Module-only delivery can hang when Bash is waiting on a real foreground helper.
The module signal trap is deferred behind a real foreground helper, leaving cleanup blocked.
The in-shell busy-loop fixture does not model deferred traps behind a real foreground subprocess.
Timeout and finally cleanup kill only the runner group, not the distinct module group.
POSIX-only signal and process-group APIs are unconditional on native Windows Python.
The matrix has no capability-aware handling for native Windows Python.
The release note promises reliable interrupted cleanup while known signal races and hangs remain.
A signal can arrive after background launch but before module_pid captures $!.
The focused runner has the same post-launch pre-$! capture orphan race.
Signal forwarding uses an unbounded wait when the module group does not exit.
The duplicate-signal comment is impossible under the distinct process-group topology.
Normal full-suite paths discard private-tally cleanup failures and can return green with a leak.
Module cleanup marks itself done before removal succeeds and cannot make a green run fail.
The positional fixture-state tuple permits role-specific Path values to be swapped silently.
Open primitive matrix dimensions and fallback branches allow invalid values to select the wrong row.
The matrix never exercises the post-launch pre-PID-capture race window.
No signal-resistant foreground helper row proves bounded escalation and reaping.
Rows do not assert runner status or full-suite failure-recording semantics.
No cleanup-removal failure injection proves loud non-green behavior.
The trap-restoration test does not verify caller job-control mode preservation.
Caller signal traps are restored while the boundary still owns its private tally.
